title: Frequency Maps, Counter Semantics, and Reconciliation · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent archive team receives a different authorized corpus, platform, language mix, file size, report consumer, terminal width, error policy, and delivery constraint and transfers frequency maps, counter semantics, and reconciliation into a changed inspectable frequency engine. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of inspectable frequency engine with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Build frequencies with explicit dictionary or Counter updates and explain missing-key and zero-count semantics.
Return a fresh result or an explicitly owned mutable result and prevent hidden cross-run state.
Assert that nonnegative frequencies sum to the accepted token count and preserve excluded-token evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Counter Update
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Bookbot team handles case 3149 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce an owned frequency map whose nonnegative counts reconcile to accepted tokens; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build frequencies with explicit dictionary or Counter updates and explain missing-key and zero-count semantics.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Counter Update.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Count Complexity
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-count-complexity-rzbpm2" that uses case 7292 to produce an owned frequency map whose nonnegative counts reconcile to accepted tokens. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Count Complexity.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Count Complexity into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Count Complexity, keep Count Complexity connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-count-complexity-rzbpm2
# Competency: bookbot-count-complexity.
# Case 7292: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def evidence_bookbot_count_complexity_rzbpm2(token_count: int = 12, frequency_total: int = 12, vocabulary: int = 5, negative_counts: int = 0, owned_result: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_rzbpm2 = "rzbpm2-2mpbzr-7292"
    assert evidence_variant_rzbpm2
    evidence_axes_rzbpm2 = (
        "corpusrzbpm2",
        "policy2mpbzr",
        "revision7292",
        "commandrzbpm27292",
        "report2mpbzr7292",
        "test7292rzbpm2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_rzbpm2)) == 6
    if token_count < 0 or vocabulary < 0:
        return False, token_count, "count-shape"
    if negative_counts > 0:
        return False, negative_counts, "negative-frequency"
    if frequency_total != token_count:
        return False, frequency_total, "reconciliation"
    if not owned_result:
        return False, vocabulary, "aliased-result"
    assert vocabulary <= token_count
    return True, vocabulary, "reconciled-counter"
